errors.description: The `mfa.require_challenge_complete` middleware refused because the caller has MFA enrolled + a role requiring MFA but no challenge has succeeded in the current session. Response includes the initiation URL.

The `mfa.require_recent_challenge` middleware refused because the last successful MFA challenge is older than `mfa.step_up.sensitive_action_ttl_minutes`. Response includes the step-up URL + the action ID the caller was trying to invoke.
Caller holds a role requiring MFA + the grace_days_after_enrollment window has elapsed + no MFA is enrolled. Response includes the enrolment URL.
The challenge_id path parameter did not resolve to a challenge row. Same error for unknown-challenge, expired-challenge, and consumed-challenge cases (no info leak about challenge state).
Submitted TOTP code did not match. Retryable up to challenge.max_attempts. Response includes the attempts_remaining count so the SPA can render 'N attempts left'.

Submitted WebAuthn assertion failed signature verification. Non-retryable within the same challenge — the client must restart the WebAuthn flow with a fresh challenge (browsers can't reuse assertions).
Submitted recovery code did not match ANY unconsumed hash on the Identity. Same error for wrong-code / already-consumed / no-MFA-enrolled cases (no info leak). Retryable up to challenge.max_attempts.
attempts_remaining reached 0. Caller must wait `mfa.challenge.cooldown_minutes_after_max` before issuing a fresh challenge. Response includes Retry-After header.
POST /me/mfa/totp/enroll refused because the Identity already has a TOTP secret persisted. To rotate, disable first, then re-enroll.
Round-2 confirm refused because the round-1 preview envelope is no longer in Redis (either TTL elapsed OR PurgeUnconfirmedEnrollmentsJob evicted). Caller must restart from /enroll/preview.
Round-2 confirm refused because no round-1 preview was ever issued for the caller's session. Same error shape as PREVIEW_EXPIRED (no info leak about which endpoint was called first).
Round-2 confirm: the submitted TOTP code did not match the ephemeral preview secret. Ephemeral state discarded; caller must restart from preview. NOT retryable on the same envelope.
Entitlement gate refused enrolment. Response includes the missing entitlement key (mfa_totp OR mfa_webauthn) so the SPA can route to the upgrade CTA.

Caller invoked an action gated by `mfa.require_recent_challenge` but their tenant does not hold the `mfa_step_up` entitlement. Middleware degrades to pass-through (no error surfaced to the tenant caller); this error code exists for the audit trail only.
Attestation verification failed at registration. Response includes a generic 'security check failed' message; full detail (which spec check failed) stays in the audit log.

Registration refused because the submitted credential_id already exists (globally unique). Either the user is registering the same physical key twice OR they're registering a synced-passkey that another Identity also has (in which case the security posture depends on whether cross-Identity sync is allowed — refused by default).
Registration refused because the Identity has `config('mfa.webauthn.max_credentials_per_identity')` (default 10) active credentials. User must delete an existing credential first.
Registration refused because the caller already has one WebAuthn credential AND their tenant lacks the `mfa_multiple_devices` entitlement (free tier). Response includes the upgrade CTA.
Assertion refused because the UV (user-verification) flag did not match the requested policy. E.g. server required UV=required but authenticator returned UV=false.

Assertion refused because the submitted sign_count is <= the stored value. Indicates a cloned authenticator OR a replay attack. When `mfa.sign_count_replay_hard_delete` is on (default), the credential is auto-deleted in the same request; when off, it's auto-disabled. Always logs a critical audit entry.
Assertion refused because the credential is disabled (disabled_at IS NOT NULL). Caller should choose a different credential OR contact support to re-enable / re-register.
Operation refused because it requires MFA enrolment but the Identity has none. E.g. DELETE /me/mfa/totp on an Identity without a TOTP secret.

DELETE /me/mfa/totp refused because the caller is trying to disable TOTP with a TOTP challenge (would leave them locked out). Response instructs the caller to use a recovery code OR a WebAuthn credential to verify the disable.
POST /me/mfa/recovery-codes/download refused because the Identity's recovery-code array is empty (all consumed OR not yet generated). Caller must regenerate instead.

Grant of a role listed in `mfa.required_for_roles` was refused because the target Identity has no MFA enrolled AND the grace_days_after_enrollment configuration is 0. Fired at the RBAC layer, error propagated through this namespace for consistency.
Platform-admin force-disable refused because `config('mfa.commands.require_reason')` is true and the request body lacked a `reason` field. Non-optional in staging + production.
